tools.name: tavily_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information on any topic. Use for news, facts, or data beyond your knowledge cutoff. Returns snippets and source URLs.

search_depth.description: The depth of the search. 'basic' for generic results, 'advanced' for more thorough search, 'fast' for optimized low latency with high relevance, 'ultra-fast' for prioritizing latency above all else

topic.description: The category of the search. This will determine which of our agents will be used for the search

time_range.description: The time range back from the current date to include in the search results

start_date.description: Will return all results after the specified start date. Required to be written in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

end_date.description: Will return all results before the specified end date. Required to be written in the format YYYY-MM-DD

max_results.description: The maximum number of search results to return

include_images.description: Include a list of query-related images in the response

include_image_descriptions.description: Include a list of query-related images and their descriptions in the response

include_raw_content.description: Include the cleaned and parsed HTML content of each search result

include_domains.description: A list of domains to specifically include in the search results, if the user asks to search on specific sites set this to the domain of the site

exclude_domains.description: List of domains to specifically exclude, if the user asks to exclude a domain set this to the domain of the site

country.description: Boost search results from a specific country. Must be a full country name (e.g., 'United States', 'Japan', 'Germany'). ISO country codes (e.g., 'us', 'jp') are not supported. Available only if topic is general. See https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/api-reference/search for the full list of supported countries.

include_favicon.description: Whether to include the favicon URL for each result

exact_match.description: Only return results containing the exact phrase(s) in quotes in your query

tools.name: tavily_extract
tools.description: Extract content from URLs. Returns raw page content in markdown or text format.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from

extract_depth.description: Use 'advanced' for LinkedIn, protected sites, or tables/embedded content

query.description: Query to rerank content chunks by relevance

tools.name: tavily_crawl
tools.description: Crawl a website starting from a URL. Extracts content from pages with configurable depth and breadth.

url.description: The root URL to begin the crawl

max_depth.description: Max depth of the crawl. Defines how far from the base URL the crawler can explore.

max_breadth.description: Max number of links to follow per level of the tree (i.e., per page)

limit.description: Total number of links the crawler will process before stopping

instructions.description: Natural language instructions for the crawler. Instructions specify which types of pages the crawler should return.

select_paths.description: Regex patterns to select only URLs with specific path patterns (e.g., /docs/.*, /api/v1.*)

select_domains.description: Regex patterns to restrict crawling to specific domains or subdomains (e.g., ^docs\.example\.com$)

allow_external.description: Whether to return external links in the final response

extract_depth.description: Advanced extraction retrieves more data, including tables and embedded content, with higher success but may increase latency

format.description: The format of the extracted web page content. markdown returns content in markdown format. text returns plain text and may increase latency.

include_favicon.description: Whether to include the favicon URL for each result

tools.name: tavily_map
tools.description: Map a website's structure. Returns a list of URLs found starting from the base URL.

url.description: The root URL to begin the mapping

max_depth.description: Max depth of the mapping. Defines how far from the base URL the crawler can explore

max_breadth.description: Max number of links to follow per level of the tree (i.e., per page)

limit.description: Total number of links the crawler will process before stopping

instructions.description: Natural language instructions for the crawler

select_paths.description: Regex patterns to select only URLs with specific path patterns (e.g., /docs/.*, /api/v1.*)

select_domains.description: Regex patterns to restrict crawling to specific domains or subdomains (e.g., ^docs\.example\.com$)

allow_external.description: Whether to return external links in the final response

tools.name: tavily_research
tools.description: Perform comprehensive research on a given topic or question. Use this tool when you need to gather information from multiple sources to answer a question or complete a task. Returns a detailed response based on the research findings. Rate limit: 20 requests per minute.

input.description: A comprehensive description of the research task

model.description: Defines the degree of depth of the research. 'mini' is good for narrow tasks with few subtopics. 'pro' is good for broad tasks with many subtopics. 'auto' automatically selects the best model.

tools.name: callees
tools.description: List functions that <symbol> calls — extracts function names from the body of the matched definition.

tools.name: callers
tools.description: Find references (call sites) to `name` across the workspace.

tools.name: context
tools.description: Read a window of lines around a given file:line position. Use after search to grab surrounding code.

tools.name: explore
tools.description: List candidate entry points: top-level directories, README files, package manifests. Cheap first step on a new repo.

tools.name: files
tools.description: List files in the workspace matching a glob pattern (supports ** recursion). Uses ripgrep (respects .gitignore).

pattern.description: glob pattern relative to workspace, e.g. "src/**/*.go",optional

tools.name: impact
tools.description: Reverse impact: which files reference `name`? Returns a sorted file list with match counts.

tools.name: search
tools.description: ripgrep-style text/regex search across the workspace. Returns matching file paths with line numbers.

max_results.description: cap on number of matches per file (default 200),optional

tools.name: trace
tools.description: Grep for `name` across the workspace; depth=1 adds 5 lines of context per match.

tools.name: ctx_execute
tools.description: Run a shell command in a sandboxed way. Heavy outputs (logs, build traces) are compressed and saved locally to prevent flooding the context window.

tools.name: ctx_index
tools.description: Index a file or directory into the local knowledge base, avoiding sending the entire file repeatedly.

path.description: file or directory path to index

tools.name: ctx_search
tools.description: Search for query terms in the indexed local knowledge base, returning only matching lines or snippets.

tools.name: ctx_stats
tools.description: Report token saving statistics of the current context virtualization session.

tools.name: spawn_agent
tools.description:
Spawn a sub-agent for a well-scoped task. Returns the spawned agent id plus the user-facing nickname when available. Spawned agents inherit your current model by default. Omit `model` to use that preferred default; set `model` only when an explicit override is needed.

Do not spawn sub-agents unless the user explicitly asks for sub-agents, delegation, or parallel agent work.
Requests for depth, thoroughness, research, investigation, or detailed codebase analysis do not count as permission to spawn.

### When to delegate vs. do the subtask yourself
- Use a subagent when a subtask is easy enough for it to handle and can run in parallel with your local work. Prefer delegating concrete, bounded sidecar tasks that materially advance the main task without blocking your immediate next local step.
- Do not delegate urgent blocking work when your immediate next step depends on that result.
- Keep work local when the subtask is too difficult to delegate well.

### Designing delegated subtasks
- Subtasks must be concrete, well-defined, and self-contained.
- Delegated subtasks must materially advance the main task.
- Do not duplicate work between the main rollout and delegated subtasks.
- For coding tasks, prefer delegating concrete code-change worker subtasks.

### After you delegate
- Call wait_agent very sparingly. Only call wait_agent when you need the result immediately for the next critical-path step and you are blocked until it returns.
- Do not redo delegated subagent tasks yourself; focus on integrating results.
- While the subagent is running in the background, do meaningful non-overlapping work immediately.

### Parallel delegation patterns
- Run multiple independent information-seeking subtasks in parallel when you have distinct questions that can be answered independently.
- Split implementation into disjoint codebase slices and spawn multiple agents for them in parallel when the write scopes do not overlap.

message.description: Initial plain-text task for the new agent. Use either message or items.

items.description: Structured input items. Use this to pass explicit mentions (for example app:// connector paths).

type.description: Input item type: text, image, local_image, skill, or mention.

text.description: Text content when type is text.

image_url.description: Image URL when type is image.

path.description: Path when type is local_image/skill, or structured mention target such as app://<connector-id> or plugin://<plugin-name>@<marketplace-name> when type is mention.

name.description: Display name when type is skill or mention.

agent_type.description: Agent role for the new agent.

fork_context.description: True forks the current thread history into the new agent; false or omitted starts with only the initial prompt.

model.description: Model override for the new agent. Omit unless an explicit override is needed.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort override for the new agent. Omit to inherit the parent effort.

service_tier.description: Service tier override for the new agent. Omit unless explicitly requested.

tools.name: send_input
tools.description: Send a message to an existing agent. Use interrupt=true to redirect work immediately. You should reuse the agent by send_input if you believe your assigned task is highly dependent on the context of a previous task.

message.description: Legacy plain-text message to send to the agent. Use either message or items.

items.description: Structured input items. Use this to pass explicit mentions (for example app:// connector paths).

type.description: Input item type: text, image, local_image, skill, or mention.

text.description: Text content when type is text.

image_url.description: Image URL when type is image.

path.description: Path when type is local_image/skill, or structured mention target such as app://<connector-id> or plugin://<plugin-name>@<marketplace-name> when type is mention.

name.description: Display name when type is skill or mention.

interrupt.description: True interrupts the current task and handles this message immediately; false or omitted queues it.

tools.name: resume_agent
tools.description: Resume a previously closed agent by id so it can receive send_input and wait_agent calls.

tools.name: wait_agent
tools.description: Wait for agents to reach a final status. Completed statuses may include the agent's final message. Returns empty status when timed out. Once the agent reaches a final status, a notification message will be received containing the same completed status.

targets.description: Agent ids to wait on. Pass multiple ids to wait for whichever finishes first.

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 600000, min 10000, max 3600000. Prefer longer waits (minutes) to avoid busy polling.

tools.name: close_agent
tools.description: Close an agent and any open descendants when they are no longer needed, and return the target agent's previous status before shutdown was requested. Completed agents remain open and count toward the concurrency limit until closed. Don't keep agents open for too long if they are not needed anymore.
